case_name: Sampurna Behera vs State of Odisha
summary: Bail is rule, jail exception - for bailable offences
key_holding: For offences punishable with imprisonment up to 3 years, bail is the rule and refusal is the exception. Accused must be granted bail unless there are strong reasons.
impact: Established presumption in favor of bail
full_text: The presumption is always in favor of granting bail to an accused charged with a bailable offence. The burden is on the prosecution to show why bail should be denied.
